key_insight: A ZrO2–Al2O3 binary activating flux makes A-TIG welding produce a deeper, narrower penetration than conventional TIG on AISI 304 stainless steel.
engineering_relevance: This approach can reduce the need for multiple passes and improve efficiency in medium-thickness stainless-steel welding.
method: Bead-on-plate welds were made on AISI 304 using automated TIG and A-TIG under identical process settings.
A binary oxide flux of zirconium dioxide (ZrO2) and aluminium oxide (Al2O3) was applied for the activated condition.
Weld geometry was measured by penetration depth, bead width, and depth-to-width ratio after metallographic preparation.
evidence_signal: Comparative measurements showed that the binary-flux A-TIG welds had greater penetration and a narrower bead profile than conventional TIG welds.
watch_item: Results are based on a specific flux mix and a single stainless-steel grade, so performance may not transfer directly to other alloys, thicknesses, or welding settings.
paper_title: COMPARATIVE INVESTIGATIONS ON THE WELD DEPTH OF PENETRATION IN ACTIVATED TIG WELDING OF STAINLESS STEEL THROUGH THE USE OF BINARY FLUX MIXTURE
